title: Is Binary Tree Symmetric?
description: A binary tree is symmetric (mirror of itself) if the left subtree is the mirror of the right subtree. Given a binary tree encoded as a level-order array ('N' for null nodes), determine if it is symmetric.
inputSpec: A single line: the level-order encoding of the tree. 'N' means null/absent node. Example: 1 2 2 3 4 4 3
outputSpec: YES if the tree is symmetric, NO otherwise.

botCodes.code:
vals=input().split()
def isMirror(l,r):
    if l>=len(vals) or r>=len(vals):return True
    if vals[l]=='N' and vals[r]=='N':return True
    if vals[l]=='N' or vals[r]=='N':return False
    if vals[l]!=vals[r]:return False
    return isMirror(2*l+1,2*r+2) and isMirror(2*l+2,2*r+1)
print('YES' if isMirror(1,2) else 'NO')
